---
title: Pixi tasks
---

Pixi tasks are named commands defined by a repository. They hide details such
as the Quarto working directory, profile arguments, environment selection, or
pre-render setup.

## Inspect available tasks

The available tasks for a project/repository can be listed with the command 

```default
pixi task list
```
Producing something similar to 

```
-----------------------------------
preview, render
Task     Description
preview  Preview the quarto site
render   Render the quarto site
```

More information about the available tasks can be found in the specific project documentation
and the `pixi.toml`-file. 

## Run a task

Run a documented task with:

```bash
pixi run <task-name>
```
Some documentation uses the equivalent abbreviation `pixi r <task-name>`.

## Frequent task roles

| Role | Typical task names | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Local authoring | `preview` | Watch source changes and serve a local preview |
| Final build | `render`, `render-all` | Produce one or all rendered outputs |
| Cleanup | `clean` | Remove previously generated material before rebuilding |
| Setup | `github-auth`, font or package fetch tasks | Provide project-specific prerequisites |
| Validation | figure, download, or script checks | Detect broken referenced resources |

## Project specific tasks

Sometimes it's useful to define a project specific task. These should be defined using tooling that 
is available in the Pixi environment if possible, to ensure that collaborators can run them without 
having to install additional softare. 

For example, a task can run a Python script using a Pixi installed Python environment following a 
recipe like 

1. Write a Python script, e.g. in `scripts/my_task_script.py`
2. Add it as a task to `pixi.toml`
3. Run using `pixi run my-task`

The second step involves adding a section like the one shown below to the `pixi.toml` file

```
[tasks.my-task]
cmd = "python scripts/my_task_script.py"
description = "Run custom Python script task."
```

See the [Pixi task documentation](https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/workspace/advanced_tasks/) for more information.

## Examples of project-specific tasks

The structural biology site defines checks for referenced figures, downloads,
and PyMOL scripts:

```bash
pixi run check-figures
pixi run check-downloads
pixi run check-pymol-scripts
```

A notebook-producing project may instead render several profiles and rename or
copy outputs for use in Colab. Such helpers are not general Quarto commands;
use them only in the repository where they are configured.

::: {.callout-warning title="Do not infer task arguments"}
New projects should expose `student` and `instructor` profile arguments and
configure visibility using `teaching.show-solutions`. Existing repositories
may use `solution` or `master` for answer-bearing output; read their task
definitions before running or documenting a build.
:::
